food_subgroup: Processed poultry products — breaded and fried chicken with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken nugget' class (FOODON_03315091). The upsized meal with fries and drink is a composite; the nugget component is matched at closest_parent level. No exact FoodOn class exists for the full meal combination.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken nuggets — breaded, fried poultry product
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers breaded fried chicken products. The full upsized meal (nuggets + fries + drink) is a composite not directly represented; nugget component matched at closest_parent level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept identified for chicken nugget meal composite.

main_ingredients: carbonated soft drink or orange juice

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (6 McNuggets + medium/large fries + medium/large drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of 6 Chicken McNuggets (approximately 105 g), a medium-to-large portion of french fries (approximately 150 g), and a medium-to-large soft drink (approximately 295 g / 295 ml), totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's USA Nutrition Information — Chicken McNuggets and French Fries; USDA FoodData Central — Chicken nuggets, breaded, frozen, prepared (FDC ID 748967) and French fries, fast food (FDC ID 170720)
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Corporation Nutrition Centre / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g values are derived by combining McDonald's published nutrition data for 6-piece Chicken McNuggets (105 g, 280 kcal) and medium french fries (117 g, 320 kcal) and a medium Coca-Cola (approximately 330 ml, ~140 kcal). Weighted composite per-100g values calculated across the full ~550 g meal. USDA FoodData Central used to cross-validate micronutrient estimates. Soft drink contribution to macros is primarily sugar and water; it dilutes the per-100g density of the composite. Values represent the full upsized meal composite.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — French fries / potato products and breaded chicken analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's 6-piece McNuggets Upsized Meal as a composite. GI estimated as a weighted average: french fries GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, baked/fried potato products), breaded chicken nuggets GI ~46 (analogous to breaded fried chicken, Atkinson et al. 2008), soft drink (cola) GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution across components: fries ~35 g carbs, nuggets ~15 g carbs, soft drink ~43 g carbs. Composite GI ≈ 65 (medium). GL per serving = (GI × total carbohydrate g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 93.5) / 100 ≈ 61.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from processed chicken, batter, and fries
hypertension.guidance: The full upsized meal delivers approximately 1,073 mg of sodium (≈195 mg per 100 g × 550 g), representing nearly 47% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal and request reduced-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from refined-carbohydrate fries and sugar-sweetened soft drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: The upsized meal carries an estimated glycemic load of approximately 61 per serving, driven by the high-GI french fries and sugar-sweetened beverage; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute the soft drink with water or unsweetened tea and consider a smaller fries portion to substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium from deep-fried ultra-processed components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The meal contains saturated fat from the chicken batter and frying oil, trace trans fats, and high sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with existing CVD or elevated LDL cholesterol should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence and pair it with increased physical activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's Chicken McNuggets 6-Piece Upsized Meal is a popular fast-food combo meal served at McDonald's restaurants in Singapore and globally. The meal consists of six bite-sized pieces of white-meat chicken that have been minced, shaped, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The nuggets are served alongside an upsized portion of McDonald's classic salted french fries — thin-cut potato strips that are par-fried, frozen, and finish-fried in-store — and a medium-to-large carbonated soft drink, typically Coca-Cola or a similar beverage. The nuggets have a crispy, lightly seasoned exterior with a tender, moist chicken interior. The meal is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal is energy-dense, delivering approximately 740–800 kcal per serving, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (fries and soft drink), moderate protein from the chicken, and fat from the deep-frying process. The sodium content is high, making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is elevated due to the high-GI french fries and sugar-sweetened beverage, posing a risk for postprandial hyperglycaemia in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content from deep-frying are relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. This meal is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet.
common_user_queries: How many calories in McDonald's McNuggets upsized meal?
Is McDonald's McNuggets meal halal in Singapore?
Nutrition facts for 6 piece McNuggets meal
How much sodium in McDonald's nuggets meal?
Is McNuggets meal suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from McDonald's published nutrition information (US/Singapore) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central for component foods. The per-100g composite values are calculated from weighted meal components and represent a reasonable estimate. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature as no direct GI measurement for this composite meal exists. Confidence is medium rather than high because: (1) exact Singapore-specific McDonald's nutrition data may differ marginally from US published values; (2) the upsized meal is a composite requiring weighted calculation rather than a direct database lookup; (3) GI is estimated rather than directly measured.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae), B-vitamins (vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg), folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the McDonald's published nutrition panel for this meal combination and cannot be reliably disaggregated from USDA component data for the composite meal without introducing unacceptable estimation error. The upsized meal in Singapore (GD000149) is served at McDonald's Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay locations per field survey data. Price is SGD 9.85. The meal is halal-certified per McDonald's Singapore halal certification. Cuisine tags from source: Burger, Fast Food, Western, Halal, Fried Chicken, Fries. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional field survey title to a canonical English form. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate based on McDonald's published component weights: 6 McNuggets ≈ 105 g, medium fries ≈ 117 g, medium soft drink ≈ 330 ml (approximately 330 g including ice dilution effect, conservatively estimated at 295 g net beverage weight for nutrient calculation). Total ≈ 517–550 g; 550 g used as the working serving size.
